goal: Rebuild daily.html to match the design document's two daily-page screens (ScreenDailyBefore + ScreenCardBoardDaily) as a state machine on the existing 2-page architecture. State 3 (ScreenDailyAfter, the post-reveal interpretation) remains on /reading.html where it is already styled by css/reading.css.
done: Rewrote the <main> block in daily.html and th/daily.html to host two discrete <section.daily-state> wrappers (State 1 = .daily-state--before, State 2 = .daily-state--board). State 1 markup: top bar (Today / วันนี้), placeholder streak strip (Day 14 + bilingual subtitle + 7-cell gold-fill bar), deck label (From the deck / Velvet Familiar / เวลเวต แฟมิเลียร์), big card-back component (.daily-bigcard with __aura halo, __card surface, __monogram M, __wordmark MEOWTAROT), ceremony copy (Tap to reveal / แตะเพื่อเปิดไพ่ + EN-only / TH-only locale-specific hint), and the gold Draw button. State 2 markup: top bar (deck label centered + streak chip right), board title (Choose your card / เลือกไพ่ของคุณ + locale-specific hint), preserved #daily-board <div> for js/main.js setupBoard, preserved #daily-counter and #daily-continue for the existing selection logic, plus a Continue · ต่อไป CTA. State machine: .daily-shell carries data-daily-phase='before' on load; one new line in js/main.js renderDaily()'s dealShuffleBtn.onclick flips it to 'board' after dailyBoard.render() runs, and css/daily.css §2 keys section visibility off the attribute. No DOM rewriting needed for the swap.
affects: meowtarot.com/daily.html and meowtarot.com/th/daily.html — homepage 'Draw today's card' CTA now lands users on State 1 (ceremonial single card-back + streak strip + Draw button) instead of the empty 12-slot board. Tapping Draw transitions to State 2 (the redesigned 12-slot selection grid with title, deck-label header, streak chip). Selection + Continue still routes to /reading.html where renderDaily() shows the revealed card result (unchanged). The dealShuffleBtn-as-deal-trigger semantics is preserved: clicking the button still calls dailyBoard.render(); the only addition is the data-daily-phase flip. /today/ also has data-page='daily' but its markup does not include .daily-shell, so the State 1/State 2 sections never render there and css/daily.css §2 is a no-op on that page — confirmed by reading the .daily-shell attribute-selector chain in §2.
notes: Streak data (Day 14 + 5-of-7 filled bar) is a hardcoded placeholder in the markup, matching the design doc's static value. Filed for follow-up wiring (read streak_current from localStorage 'meowtarot_user_progress' and render dynamically — same data source used by js/profile.js). Deck name 'Velvet Familiar / เวลเวต แฟมิเลียร์' is also placeholder — should come from js/data.js DECKS[activeDeckId] when the deck-switcher UI lands. The State 1 'Draw card' button reuses the existing id='daily-deal-shuffle' so the existing handler binding in js/main.js renderDaily() continues to attach without rewiring. Per memory feedback_selection_ux_tap_to_confirm, the Continue button in State 2 still requires explicit tap (no auto-advance on 1/1). Per memory project_meowtarot_data_page_collision, /today/ shares data-page='daily' but its markup lacks .daily-shell so the CSS state-machine is a no-op there. css/daily.css and css/theme-tokens.css: two design-doc bg colors #ead4ee + #efd0e2 were added as a single linear-gradient token --mt-bg-gradient-daily; the post-reveal variant --mt-bg-gradient-daily-after was also pre-added for the future reading.css daily-mode result page. NO PUSH per user instruction — local server still running on port 8000 from the Phase 5 homepage push, serving the new daily.html for review at http://localhost:8000/daily.html.
verified: grep data-daily-phase js/main.js: 2 matches (1 setAttribute call + 1 comment)

markup class presence: daily-state--before, daily-state--board, daily-streak-strip, daily-bigcard, daily-ceremony, daily-deck-label, daily-topbar--board, daily-board-title all present in both EN and TH
css/daily.css hex literals: zero (initial draft had #ead4ee + #efd0e2 in the page bg; swapped to var(--mt-bg-gradient-daily) and the values moved into css/theme-tokens.css as a token)
md5 canonical == www == ios for daily.html, th/daily.html, js/main.js, css/daily.css, css/theme-tokens.css
cap sync ios completed; no plugin errors
local http://localhost:8000/daily.html returns the new daily-bigcard / daily-state--before / daily-streak-strip / daily-deck-label markup
filed: Streak strip wiring — currently hardcoded 'Day 14 streak' + 5/7 filled bar. Wire to localStorage 'meowtarot_user_progress'.streak_current + read the same milestone logic js/profile.js uses for the 7-day visual. Apply on State 1 streak strip (.daily-streak-strip) and State 2 streak chip (.daily-topbar__streak-num + .daily-topbar__streak-label).

Deck name wiring — currently hardcoded 'Velvet Familiar / เวลเวต แฟมิเลียร์'. Wire to DECKS[activeDeckId].name and DECKS[activeDeckId].name_th from js/data.js. Two display points: .daily-deck-label__name on State 1 and .daily-topbar__deck-name on State 2. Locked-deck variants of the big card-back (.daily-bigcard__card with locked filter) not implemented this round.

Big card-back monogram is a static 'M' in CSS; design doc's per-deck card backs would use different monograms / colors per deck. Tied to the broader 'Card-back asset redesign' item filed in 2026-05-28-phase32-cardslot-fallback-fixup.

State 1 'Today' date is a static label. Future commit should render today's actual date (locale-formatted) plus the day-of-week. Hook into js/common.js dict or use Intl.DateTimeFormat with state.currentLang.
